description:
GOAL: Per-fn opt-out from the inliner via an `@no_inline` attribute on the
AST `fn` form, threaded through AST → ir_lower → FnIr → ir_inline.

Status when authored: speculative. Open in the original fz-ul4.11.15 design
("Per-fn opt-out attribute (e.g. @no_inline)?"). Defer until we have a
concrete case where the inliner makes a fn slower or harder to debug.

## When this becomes load-bearing

  - A fn whose body is small and leaf, but inlining it explodes a hot caller's
    code size beyond what GROWTH_CAP catches.
  - A fn we want to keep distinct for profiling/debuggability.
  - A fn whose semantics must not be observed via the inlined merged frame
    (e.g., specialized for tier-up — though that's also handled by .11.24.7's
    specialize_return path).

## Sketch of work

  1. AST: extend Item::FnDecl (src/ast.rs) with an `attrs: Vec<Attr>` field;
     parser recognizes `@no_inline` before a `fn` head.
  2. ir_lower: propagate flag to FnIr (new field `pub no_inline: bool` on
     FnIr; default false).
  3. ir_inline: gate `is_inlinable` on `!f.no_inline`.
  4. Tests: a marked fn is not inlined even when otherwise eligible.

## Acceptance

  - One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.25.

## References

  - fz-ul4.11.15 parent (deferral list).
  - src/ir_inline.rs::is_inlinable (created by .11.15.1).
title: @no_inline attribute (per-fn opt-out)
